name: Asteroid
description: Small rocky objects that orbit the Sun, found primarily in the asteroid belt.

name: Black Hole
description: A region in space where the gravitational pull is so strong that nothing, not even light, can escape.

name: Comet
description: Icy celestial objects that develop tails when they approach the Sun.

name: Dwarf Planet
description: Celestial bodies that orbit the Sun but are not large enough to be considered full-fledged planets.

name: Exoplanet
description: Planets that orbit stars outside our solar system.

name: Galaxy
description: Vast systems of stars, gas, dust, and dark matter held together by gravity.

name: Hubble Space Telescope
description: A space telescope that has provided stunning images and valuable data about the universe.

name: Interstellar
description: Relating to the space between stars or the journey between star systems.

name: Jupiter
description: The largest planet in our solar system, known for its massive size and swirling storms.

name: Kuiper Belt
description: A region beyond Neptune in our solar system containing many small icy bodies.

name: Light-Year
description: The distance that light travels in one year, often used to measure cosmic distances.

name: Mars
description: The fourth planet from the Sun, often referred to as the 'Red Planet.'

name: Neutron Star
description: The collapsed core of a massive star, incredibly dense and primarily composed of neutrons.

name: Orbit
description: The path that an object, such as a planet or moon, follows as it revolves around another object in space.

name: Pulsar
description: A highly magnetized, rotating neutron star that emits beams of electromagnetic radiation.

name: Quasar
description: Extremely bright and distant objects in the universe, powered by supermassive black holes.

name: Red Dwarf
description: Small and cool stars that are the most common type of star in the universe.

name: Supernova
description: The explosive death of a massive star, resulting in a bright burst of energy.

name: Telescope
description: An optical instrument that gathers and magnifies distant objects, used for observing celestial bodies.

name: Uranus
description: The seventh planet from the Sun, known for its tilted axis and unique blue-green color.

name: Venus
description: The second planet from the Sun, often called Earth's 'sister planet.'

name: White Dwarf
description: The remaining core of a star after it has exhausted its nuclear fuel.

name: X-Ray Astronomy
description: The study of the universe using X-ray emissions from celestial objects.

name: Yuri Gagarin
description: The first human to travel into space, a Soviet astronaut.

name: Zodiac
description: A belt of constellations through which the Sun, Moon, and planets appear to move across the sky.
